calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($1,934,000 USD / 160 total beneficiaries) = $12,087.50 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 0.0827 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.elaboration: ["Quote: The Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage (TNRM) programme was launched on 10 November 2018. This TNRM programme is a collaboration between Animal Veterinary Service (AVS), Animal Welfare Groups (AWGS), and other relevant stakeholders. The intent of this programme is that free-roaming dogs will be sterilised after capture, and subsequently rehomed. Those that cannot be rehomed will be released at suitable locations to live out their lives naturally. Through our Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage (TNRM) program, we stepped in where few could, offering swift intervention, medical care, and a pathway to recovery.", "Highest tractability intervention found: 'Policy And Legal Advocacy'", "EA Rationale: Policy and legal advocacy can create systemic, legally-binding protections for animals, representing a highly tractable path to impact."]
details.formula: Mapping of charity's intervention types against the EA Intervention Tractability evidence base.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs supported in shelter, foster homes, and strays fed in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: Mercylight takes care of about 160 dogs, in the shelter, via fosterers as well as through stray feeding. This year, 160 lives were changed—each one a dog once forgotten, now seen, loved, and cared for.
metrics.source_citation: Mercylight Animal Rescue and Sanctuary Annual Report 2024, p. 5, 6

metrics.context_qualifier: Total man-hours contributed by 110 volunteers annually in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: Our volunteers contribute about 24,300 man-hours annually, averaging 5 hours per week. Nearly 85% of our volunteers contribute an average of 5 hours each week, amounting to an extraordinary 24,300 man-hours of service annually.
metrics.source_citation: Mercylight Animal Rescue and Sanctuary Annual Report 2024, p. 5, 10

metrics.context_qualifier: Individuals from tertiary institutions participated in community engagement initiatives in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: 540 individuals from tertiary institutions dedicated their time to community engagement initiatives at our shelter. In total, 540 volunteers from various organisations participated in our programs across different months in 2024.
metrics.source_citation: Mercylight Animal Rescue and Sanctuary Annual Report 2024, p. 5, 9

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs rescued in distress in 2024 through the Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage (TNRM) program.
metrics.evidence_quote: We rescued 14 dogs in need. In 2024, our dedicated team responded to urgent calls for help, rescuing 14 dogs in distress.
metrics.source_citation: Mercylight Animal Rescue and Sanctuary Annual Report 2024, p. 5, 7

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs that found loving forever homes in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: rehome 12 dogs. In 2024, 12 dogs found their forever homes—each one a joyful new chapter.
metrics.source_citation: Mercylight Animal Rescue and Sanctuary Annual Report 2024, p. 5, 8

significant_events.source_quote: The Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage (TNRM) programme was launched on 10 November 2018. This TNRM programme is a collaboration between Animal Veterinary Service (AVS), Animal Welfare Groups (AWGS), and other relevant stakeholders. The intent of this programme is that free-roaming dogs will be sterilised after capture, and subsequently rehomed. Those that cannot be rehomed will be released at suitable locations to live out their lives naturally. Through our Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage (TNRM) program, we stepped in where few could, offering swift intervention, medical care, and a pathway to recovery.
significant_events.summary: Participation in a nationwide program to sterilize, rehome, or release stray dogs, offering swift intervention and medical care.

significant_events.source_quote: Mercylight participated in numerous adoption drives and community engagement to promote a kinder society to animals. 540 individuals from tertiary institutions dedicated their time to community engagement initiatives at our shelter. In total, 540 volunteers from various organisations participated in our programs across different months in 2024. These sessions allowed them to interact with our dogs, assist with shelter tasks, and gain deeper insight into animal welfare, rescue, and rehabilitation.
significant_events.summary: Engaged 540 individuals, including 160 students, in educational sessions and shelter tasks to foster animal welfare understanding.

significant_events.source_quote: We are advocating stricter laws to safeguard the interest of animals. It's a long journey with the authorities and progressing slowly. Public Policy Influence: Efforts to influence public policy related to responsible pet ownership, animal breeding, and humane stray animal management.
significant_events.summary: Advocating for stricter laws to safeguard animal interests and influence public policy related to pet ownership, breeding, and stray management.

significant_events.source_quote: We became a Project ADORE Partner, strengthening our commitment to promoting responsible pet ownership and adoption.
significant_events.summary: Became a Project ADORE Partner, strengthening commitment to promoting responsible pet ownership and adoption.

significant_events.source_quote: "Rescue, Heal, Restore Homeless Dogs" Campaign under Enhanced Fund-Raising Program by Tote Board with government dollar-for-dollar matching. 1 September 2024 - 31 March 2025. "Be A Blessing, Make A Difference" Campaign under Enhanced Fund-Raising Program by Tote Board with government dollar-for-dollar matching. 1 January - 31 March 2024.
significant_events.summary: Conducted 'Rescue, Heal, Restore Homeless Dogs' and 'Be A Blessing, Make A Difference' campaigns with government matching.

significant_events.source_quote: In August 2024, we gave back to those who give so much. Volunteers and our furry companions enjoyed a joyous staycation at CSC @ Loyang's dog-friendly bungalow—a day filled with laughter, play, and connection.
significant_events.summary: Hosted a staycation for volunteers and dogs in August 2024 to foster bonding and community.

data.original_text: We started in 2009 as a handful of volunteers who fed stray dogs every evening in the eastern side of Singapore.

Mercylight was incorporated in January 2015 as a non-profit organization with the aim to promote compassion for animals through the provision of animal welfare services and prevention of cruelty to animals.
We obtained the Institution of Public Character (IPC) status on 24 January 2022.
Thiswas a great milestone for MARS. With strong financial controlsand processes in place, we achieved IPC Status in 18 months fromCharity Status.
To date we have over 100 committed people volunteering as kennel volunteers, stray feeders, packers, fosterers and sponsors.
Animal rescue work is obviously not an easy task. There are about 7,000 stray dogs out there suffering from hunger, thirst, harsh weather conditions and unkind treatment.

We are part of the nationwide initiative launched in November 2018, known as the Trap-Neuter-Release-Manage (TNRM) programme with the aim of sterilising more than 70 per cent of the stray dog population over five years.
According to AVA, approximately 15,000 to 20,000 cats and dogs are put down yearly in Singapore because the shelters are full and there is no place to house the impounded animals.
To improve the quality of lives of stray dogs by rescuing, rehabilitating and re-homing them.

Mercylight is fully run by volunteers, with some volunteers coming almost every day to help at the shelter. We have about 100 volunteers, that keep the shelter running. The volunteers help to walk, feed and administer medication to the dogs twice a day.

As of 2022, our organization was providing care and support for a total of 120 dogs. These dogs are distributed across various locations, including our shelter, foster homes, as well as strays that we are feeding and attending to.
In 2022, our dedicated team at Mercylight successfully rescued 32 adult dogs and puppies.

Our rescue efforts extend beyond physical care to encompass behavioral training and socialization, ensuring each dog is prepared for their transition to a loving forever home. 16 dogs found good homes in 2022.
We became a Project ADORE Partner, strengthening our commitment to promoting responsible pet ownership and adoption.

Through our Community. Engagement Program, we provided educational sessions to 160 students from the National University of Singapore, fostering a deeper understanding of animal welfare issues and the vital role animal shelters play in our society.
Your tax-deductible donation provides critical medical care, and your gift is eligible for a 2.5x tax deduction.

Every dollar you donate to our campaign will be matched by Tote Board and the Singapore government under the Enhanced Fund-Raising (EFR) Programme. Your donation will have double the impact, providing critical care for dogs in desperate need. Additionally, it will also cover the essential operational expenses of our shelter.
